summary:
Claim: Gutta-percha obturation in primary teeth is indicated only when the permanent successor is missing.  

Evidence: - Standard guidelines (AAPD/WHO) recommend avoiding obturation unless a successor is absent to preserve space and prevent developmental issues. - Absent successors increase risk of pulpal disease progression and structural compromise without intervention.  

Answer: B  

(498 tokens) ✅
